title: gymnotrix humilis
intro: Gymnotrix humilis, also known as Gymnema sylvestre, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of central and western India, and is commonly referred to as the 'gurmar plant'. It has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to regulate blood sugar levels and aid in weight loss. The health benefits of Gymnotrix humilis include its potential to reduce cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and lower blood pressure. Herbal preparations of Gymnotrix humilis are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a supplement to support overall health and well-being.
